_schema: Vextreme file lattice — one entry per file, edges define reads/writes/loads/tests. Load this file first when arriving cold: it gives you the system topology without reading any implementation. Navigate laterally (adjacent nodes in changeMap) before diving into depth (file contents). File headers carry a local LATTICE block for quick reference when you are already inside a file; this map is the canonical source if they diverge.
_usage: 1. Read this file before opening any lib/ file — understand what a file does and what it touches before reading it
2. When about to change something, look up the file's changeMap to find every adjacent node that must also be checked
3. Follow changeMap links recursively until you have visited every affected node (closed the circuit)
4. After adding or moving a file, add its node here and add it to the loadedBy/reads of its neighbors

lib/vex-config.js.role: constants hub — pure declarations, no I/O
lib/vex-config.js.context: Every semantic string in the system (category names, language codes, feature IDs, scope names, CDN paths) lives here as a named constant. No build script or widget should hardcode 'demo', 'ja', 'common', etc. Changes here propagate to 9+ files — the widest fan-out of any single file.

scopeRelPath() logic changed: widgets/fab-lang.js (applies the same rule at runtime — must stay identical or fetches 404)

lib/build-index.js.role: content pipeline assembler — reads source data, writes the single pre-built index everything reads from
lib/build-index.js.context: The pipeline hub. Every page, every arc nav render, every God Script assembly starts from data/index.json which this file produces. If the index schema changes, 4+ consumers break. Run manually or via CI after editing nodes.json or arcs-v2.json.

supportedLangs field: lib/build-vextreme.js (passes into every God Script as window.VEX_SUPPORTED_LANGS)

lib/build-vextreme.js.role: God Script assembler — reads index + string bundles, writes one self-contained dist/ file per page
lib/build-vextreme.js.context: The write side of the page delivery pipeline. Inlines EN strings, bakes in viewmodel, includes feature widgets. Once a God Script is loaded by a page, it needs zero additional fetches for EN. Run after build-index.js and strings-compile.js.
reads: lib/vextreme-index-v2.js (arc-nav feature, via srcDir: LIB_DIR in FEATURES registry)

FEATURES registry entry added: tests/08 (add coverage for new feature output)
scope bundle path convention: lib/strings-compile.js (must write to same path this reads from)
widgets/fab-lang.js (must fetch from same path at runtime)
dist/ output filenames: lib/build-sw.js (PRECACHE_URLS — sw.js must include new filenames)
pages/*.html (each page references its dist/ file by name)

lib/strings-compile.js.role: strings pipeline compiler — reads source JSON, writes compiled bundles and manifest
lib/strings-compile.js.context: The write side of the i18n pipeline. Every string key, every language bundle, every stale-detection hash is produced here. Run after editing any file in data/strings/source/. The scope bundle path rule here MUST match the runtime fetch rule in widgets/fab-lang.js.
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (reads scope bundles for God Script assembly)
widgets/fab-lang.js (fetches scope bundles at runtime for JA)

scopeRelPath() path rule: widgets/fab-lang.js (applies the same rule at runtime — must stay identical)

lib/vextreme-index-v2.js.role: browser arc nav runtime — reads index.json at load time, renders prev/next/position into #arcNavMount
lib/vextreme-index-v2.js.context: THE arc nav widget for the v2 architecture. Supersedes lib/arc-nav.js (v1, Squarespace-era, reads VEXTREME_ARCS). Inlined into God Scripts via the arc-nav feature in the FEATURES registry. Also loads standalone on non-God-Script pages via shell.js.
reads: window.VEX_STRINGS_EN (God Script fast path — already inlined by build-vextreme.js)
testedBy: tests/03-browser-nav.test.js (data logic; no browser render tests yet)

urlFromSlug() URL construction: pages/*.html filenames (must match the URLs this generates)
#arcNavMount selector: every page HTML that uses arc-nav must have <div id="arcNavMount">

lib/arc-nav.js.role: v1 Squarespace-era arc nav — DO NOT USE for God Script pages
lib/arc-nav.js.context: The original arc nav widget, designed for squarespace-injection.html. Reads from window.VEXTREME_ARCS (old arcs.json format), not index.json. Kept for reference and the Squarespace deployment path. For all GitHub Pages / God Script pages, use lib/vextreme-index-v2.js instead.
reads: window.PAGE_ARCS (set per-page by the embedding loader)

data/nodes.json.role: content node source — the authoritative list of all content slugs
data/nodes.json.context: Every piece of content is a node here. The slug is the system's only identifier — arc membership, string scope keys, HTML filenames, and God Script names all derive from it. This is the write side; data/index.json is the read side.
loadedBy: lib/build-index.js (primary consumer — reads on every build)

slug added: data/arcs-v2.json (add to arc membership if it belongs to an arc)
data/strings/source/ (create scope file for the page's strings)
data/viewmodels.json (add override if not production/page defaults)
slug renamed: see pe-006: lib/rename-slug.js tooling planned but not yet built

data/arcs-v2.json.role: arc structure source — defines arc membership, ordering, and metadata
data/arcs-v2.json.context: Arcs are the navigation layer: ordered groups of content nodes with display metadata. The v2 format uses slug-keyed entries (not URL-keyed). build-index.js reads this alongside nodes.json to produce arcMap and arcMeta in index.json.

arc entry added: data/nodes.json (all slugs in the arc must exist there)

data/viewmodels.json.role: per-slug viewmodel overrides — demo/specimen pages that aren't content nodes
data/viewmodels.json.context: Content nodes in nodes.json get default viewmodels (production/page template). This file holds overrides for demo and specimen pages that have different categories, templates, or feature sets. Also used to add non-node slugs (demo pages, specimens) to the God Script assembly pipeline.

slug entry added/changed: lib/build-vextreme.js (rebuild God Script for that slug)
data/strings/source/ (must have compiled scope bundle for assembly to succeed)

data/index.json.role: pre-built read index — the single artifact every page and build script reads from
data/index.json.context: Generated by lib/build-index.js. Never edit directly. Contains slugMap (slug → viewmodel + arcKeys + nav data), arcMap (arc → sections → slug lists), arcMeta (arc → display metadata), supportedLangs, nodeCount, arcCount, builtAt. Everything downstream reads this; nothing downstream reads nodes.json or arcs-v2.json directly.
loadedBy: lib/vextreme-index-v2.js (browser fetches via CDN, caches in localStorage)

schema changes: lib/build-index.js (producer — update to emit new schema)

widgets/fab-lang.js.role: browser language switcher FAB — fetches JA scope bundles, applies [data-i18n] swaps
widgets/fab-lang.js.context: The runtime i18n engine. On language switch, constructs scope bundle URLs using the same scopeRelPath rule as strings-compile.js (build time) — these MUST stay in sync. Caches fetched JA strings in localStorage keyed by version + sorted scope list. Reads VEX_STRING_SCOPES, VEX_STRING_CATEGORY from window (set by God Script).
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (inlined as lang feature in God Scripts)
legacy pages via direct CDN script tag (pre-God-Script pattern)

localStorage key format: any future offline FAB that reads cache state (pe-008)
VEX_* global names read: lib/build-vextreme.js assembleGodScript() (must emit matching global names)

widgets/sw-register.js.role: Service Worker registration — activates offline caching on first page load
widgets/sw-register.js.context: Core module: default: true in the FEATURES registry, inlined into every God Script. Registers sw.js. Once active, the SW pre-caches all God Scripts + core assets at install time — subsequent page loads make zero network requests for HTML or JS. Without this, the offline/caching pyramid is inactivated.
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (inlined as core module in every God Script, default: true)
testedBy: tests/09-build-sw.test.js (tests sw.js generation, not this file directly)

SW registration scope or path: sw.js (must exist at the registered path)

sw.js.role: Service Worker — pre-caches all God Scripts and core assets at install
sw.js.context: Generated by lib/build-sw.js. Pre-caches every dist/vextreme-{slug}.js file plus core CSS/JSON assets. After install, page visits cost zero network requests for cached assets. Stale SW caches are cleaned up on activate. Never edit directly — regenerate with node lib/build-sw.js.
loadedBy: widgets/sw-register.js (registers this file in every God Script page)

PRECACHE_URLS list: lib/build-sw.js (generates this list from dist/ filenames)
run: node lib/build-sw.js after adding any new dist/ file

lib/build-sw.js.role: Service Worker generator — reads dist/ filenames, writes sw.js
lib/build-sw.js.context: Scans dist/ for God Script filenames and bakes them into the SW PRECACHE_URLS list. Run after build-vextreme.js adds new dist/ files. The generated sw.js is committed — the SW cache version is pinned to the git commit hash at generation time.

CORE_ASSETS list: styles/design-system.css, styles/arc-nav.css, data/index.json (must exist at these paths)
cache name format: sw.js activate handler (cleans caches by name pattern)

blueprint.json.role: system blueprint — canonical registry of all feature IDs, category IDs, template IDs
blueprint.json.context: The configuration contract. Every Feature constant in vex-config.js must have a matching entry here, and every entry here must have a config file in config/features/. Tests/06 enforces this. Adding a new feature to FEATURES registry in build-vextreme.js without adding it here will fail the blueprint tests.
